LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS CATHERINE CHOW, CORINA NURIMBA, WILSON CHOW, DAVID LIU & AZALEA BOUTIQUE, INC.'S MOTION FOR RELIEF FROM DEFAULT IS GRANTED. DEFENDANTS HAVE DEMONSTRATED THAT THEY ARE ENTITLED TO MANDATORY RELIEF. (CCP 473.) ALTHOUGH ATTORNEYS WOO AND COHEN ATTEMPT TO "DEFLECT FAULT" FROM THEMSELVES, THEIR DECLARATIONS HAVE THE "OPPOSITE EFFECT" AND MEET THE REQUIREMENTS FOR MANDATORY RELIEF UNDER CCP 473(B). CARMEL LTD. V. TAVOUSSI (2009) 175 CAL. APP. 4TH 393, 401. THERE IS NO EVIDENCE OF NEGLECT OR WRONGDOING BY DEFENSE COUNSELS' CLIENTS. PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL IS ENTITLED TO REASONABLE ATTORNEY FEES IN AN AMOUNT TO BE DECIDED AT A FUTURE MOTION. DEFENDANT TO FILE THE ANSWER THAT IS ATTACHED TO THE MOVING PAPER. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185

ANY PREJUDICE TO DEFENDANTS WAS OBVIATED BY THE COURT CONTINUING THE MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT AND PERMITTING DEFENDANTS TO FILE A REPLY. JUDGE: MARLA J. RECEIVED REQUEST FOR ENTRY OF DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT FILED BY PLAINTIFF TOBI.COM, LLC, A DELAWARE LIMITED LIABILITY COMPANY AS TO DEFENDANT AZALEA BOUTIQUE, INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF TOBI.COM, LLC'S MOTION TO DISQUALIFY JOSHUA COHEN AS COUNSEL FOR DEFENDANTS. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. THE COURT GRANTS THE MOTION TO DISQUALIFY UNDER KENNEDY V. ELDRIDGE (2011) 201 CAL.APP.4TH 1197. THE COURT FINDS THAT THERE IS A SIGNIFICANT DANGER THAT'AS A RESULT OF MR. COHEN'S PRIOR ATTORNEY-CLIENT RELATIONSHIP WITH MR. ENGLISH (PLAINTIFF'S FORMER CHAIRMAN)'MR. COHEN ACQUIRED RELEVANT CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION ABOUT PLAINTIFF TO WHICH HE OTHERWISE WOULD NOT HAVE HAD ACCESS. THAT CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION IS SUBSTANTIALLY RELATED TO THE INSTANT DISPUTE, AND WOULD LIKELY BE USED ADVANTAGEOUSLY IN THIS LITIGATION AGAINST PLAINTIFF. FOR EXAMPLE, MR. COHEN RECEIVED TOBI.COM BOARD OF DIRECTORS MATERIALS, INCLUDING A DRAFT SETTLEMENT LETTER THAT TOBI.COM CONTEMPLATED SENDING PERTAINING TO THIS LAWSUIT AND THE SAME DEFENDANTS WHO MR. COHEN NOW REPRESENTS. (ENGLISH DECL. IN OPPOSITION TO MOTION, 3, EX. A.) MR. COHEN DISCUSSED THE SUBSTANCE OF THE JANUARY 2012 TOBI.COM BOARD MEETING (WHERE THIS LAWSUIT WAS DISCUSSED), WITH HIS THEN-CLIENT ENGLISH. ENGLISH AT THE TIME WAS ACTING IN THE CAPACITY OF CHAIRMAN OF TOBI.COM. AS ATTORNEY FOR ENGLISH, MR. COHEN REVIEWED THE DRAFT SETTLEMENT LETTER BEFORE IT WAS SENT TO HIS NOW CLIENTS. (COHEN DECL. IN OPPOSITION TO MOTION, 6). MR. COHEN, THOUGH FORMALLY ACTING AS ATTORNEY FOR BOARD CHAIR ENGLISH, WENT SO FAR AS TO WRITE AN EMAIL TO TOBI.COM COUNSEL COMMENTING ON THE PROPOSED SETTLEMENT LETTER AND ITS EFFECT ON TOBI.COM. (ID. AT 7, EX. A.) WITH THIS MR. COHEN'S CONTINUED REPRESENTATION OF DEFENDANTS IN THIS CASE UNDERMINES THE INTEGRITY OF THE JUDICIAL PROCESS. KENNEDY V. ELDRIDGE, SUPRA, 210 CAL. APP. 4TH AT 1205. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: SUSAN CROZIER, CSR # 4761
SUBSTITUTION OF ATTORNEY: JUNG, CHARLES H SUBSTITUTED FOR COHEN, JOSUA D. AS ATTORNEY FOR AZALEA BOUTIQUE, INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION

Off Calendar Jan-30-2013 Continued To Mar-27-13 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Relief From Default
Off Calendar Feb-01-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Charles H. Jung, Esq. of Nassiri & Jung LLP, Tel. 415.762.3100, for defendants and moving party;
Adam R. Bernstein, Esq. of the Law Offices of Adam R. Bernstein, Tel. 408.628.4493, for plaintiff and opposing party.

RULING: A R G U E D. At the conclusion of argument, the court modified its tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANTs CATHERINE CHOW, CORINA NURIMBA, WILSON CHOW, DAVID LIU & AZALEA BOUTIQUE, INC.'s Motion For Relief From Default is Granted. Defendants have demonstrated that they are entitled to mandatory relief. (CCP 473.) Although attorneys Woo and Cohen attempt to "deflect fault" from themselves, their declarations have the "opposite effect" and meet the requirements for mandatory relief under CCP 473(b). Carmel Ltd. v. Tavoussi (2009) 175 Cal. App. 4th 393, 401. There is no evidence of neglect or wrongdoing by defense counsels' clients. Plaintiff's counsel is entitled to reasonable attorney fees in an amount to be decided at a future motion. Defendant to file the answer that is attached to the moving paper. Order signed. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185

Mtn To Strike Pltf'S Untimely Oppos; Pos
Off Calendar Feb-01-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Charles H. Jung, Esq. of Nassiri & Jung LLP, Tel. 415.762.3100, for defendants and moving party;
Adam R. Bernstein, Esq. of the Law Offices of Adam R. Bernstein, Tel. 408.628.4493, for plaintiff and opposing party.

RULING: The court adopted the tentative ruling which was not contested. DEFENDANTs CATHERINE CHOW, CORINA NURIMBA, WILSON CHOW, DAVID LIU & AZALEA BOUTIQUE, INC.'s Motion To Strike Plaintiff'S Untimely Opposition is Denied. Any prejudice to Defendants was obviated by the court continuing the motion to set aside default and permitting Defendants to file a reply. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185

Mtn To Strike Pltf'S Untimely Oppos; Pos
Off Calendar Jan-23-2013 Continued To Feb-01-13 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Andrew R. Kislik, Esq. of Nassiri & Jung LLP, Tel. 415.762.3100, for defendants and moving party;
Kelly Matayoshi, Esq. of Farella Braun & Martel LLP, Tel. 415.954.4400, for plaintiff and opposing party.

RULING: Judge Miller disclosed that she and Mr. Kislik went to the same law school but this shall not affect her ability to be fair and impartial in this case. No objections were raised. The matter was discussed. DEFENDANT CATHERINE CHOW, CORINA NURIMBA, WILSON CHOW, ADAVID LIU and AZALEA BOUTIQUE, INC.'s Motion To Strike Plaintiff's Untimely Opposition is continued to February 1, 2013 at 9:30 am in Dept. 302 per agreement of parties. Defendant shall file its reply papers no later than 1/28/13. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Dec-21-2012 Continued To Feb-13-13 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Disqualify Joshua Cohen As Counsel For Defendants
Off Calendar Nov-27-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. ADAM R. BERNSTEIN, TEL. NO. (408) 628-4493 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF TOBI.COM, LLC'S Motion To Disqualify Joshua Cohen As Counsel For Defendants. The Court grants the motion to disqualify under Kennedy v. Eldridge (2011) 201 Cal.App.4th 1197. The Court finds that there is a significant danger that'as a result of Mr. Cohen's prior attorney-client relationship with Mr. English (Plaintiff's former Chairman)'Mr. Cohen acquired relevant confidential information about Plaintiff to which he otherwise would not have had access. That confidential information is substantially related to the instant dispute, and would likely be used advantageously in this litigation against Plaintiff. For example, Mr. Cohen received Tobi.com board of directors materials, including a draft settlement letter that Tobi.com contemplated sending pertaining to this lawsuit and the same defendants who Mr. Cohen now represents. (English Decl. in Opposition to Motion, 3, Ex. A.) Mr. Cohen discussed the substance of the January 2012 Tobi.com board meeting (where this lawsuit was discussed), with his then-client English. English at the time was acting in the capacity of Chairman of Tobi.com. As attorney for English, Mr. Cohen reviewed the draft settlement letter before it was sent to his now clients. (Cohen Decl. in Opposition to Motion, 6). Mr. Cohen, though formally acting as attorney for board chair English, went so far as to write an email to Tobi.com counsel commenting on the proposed settlement letter and its effect on Tobi.com. (Id. at 7, Ex. A.) with this Mr. Cohen's continued representation of defendants in this case undermines the integrity of the judicial process. Kennedy v. Eldridge, supra, 210 Cal. App. 4th at 1205. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Susan Crozier, CSR # 4761
